confirmations.reason: CLAUDE.md is exactly a repo/project-scoped markdown file that gives the agent standing instructions and context, loaded automatically — a direct match.

Bundled Orchestration Skills are pre-built prompt playbooks for orchestration/spawning agents, not a general mechanism to bundle and load reusable capabilities/instructions as extensions. This is adjacent (orchestration automation) rather than a packaging/loading system.

Agent Lifecycle Hooks trigger custom logic on specific agent lifecycle events (idle, task creation, task completion), which directly matches 'run custom logic on agent events before/after a tool call or on stop.'
Output Styles are saved configurations that modify Claude's system prompt and can be reused — directly shaping the agent's system prompt and output style.
Model Context Protocol (MCP) is the open-source standard for connecting external tools, data, and services to Claude Code — an exact match for the external tool/connector protocol capability.
Managed MCP Configuration directly covers deploying, configuring, and scoping a set of MCP servers with allowlists/denylists — a direct realization of install/configure/scope/discover MCP servers.
MCP Auth Methods explicitly supports env-var, HTTP header, and OAuth 2/2.1 token-based authentication to MCP servers — a direct and specific match.
MCP Tool Allow-listing provides granular permission control over which MCP tools the agent may invoke, including wildcard rules — directly realizing MCP tool governance/allow-listing.
Auto Memory (MEMORY.md) persists notes Claude writes itself across sessions, per git repository, and auto-loads them — directly realizing durable cross-session agent memory.
The Subagents feature describes specialized AI assistants running in isolated context windows handling delegated tasks and returning results — a direct match for spawning focused sub-agents.
Agent Teams coordinates multiple independent Claude Code sessions working together with a team lead and shared state — directly realizing multi-agent teams that share work and communicate.

Dynamic Workflows are JavaScript scripts Claude writes dynamically to orchestrate subagents at scale; they are generated/dynamic, not predefined repeatable named multi-step task sequences the user defines and saves. The capability describes saved/named automations, which is a different concept.
Plan Mode researches and proposes changes without executing or modifying anything — directly matching 'produce a reviewable plan before the agent edits anything.'

The Built-in Maintenance Prompt (/loop command) continues unfinished work and runs cleanup passes, but it is not a goal-directed loop with a verifiable 'done' condition the agent autonomously evaluates. It's a manually-triggered continuation command, not autonomous iteration toward a set goal.
Worktree Isolation runs parallel agents in separate git worktrees/checkouts so they don't overwrite each other — directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Claude Code on the Web runs tasks on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure accessible from browser or phone — directly realizing a managed cloud execution environment.
Background Agent Execution runs a subagent as a non-blocking background task alongside other operations — a direct match for background/async execution.
Routines are saved Claude Code configurations that run automatically on a schedule on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure — directly realizing scheduled/triggered runs.
Sandbox Runtime wraps the Claude Code process in Seatbelt/bubblewrap isolation to limit blast radius — a direct realization of sandboxed execution.
The Permission System classifies tools by approval requirements and governs whether Claude Code may use them — directly realizing tool permission controls (approve/deny what the agent can do).
Egress Firewall Control restricts outbound network traffic to explicitly allowed domains — a direct and specific match for network/egress control.
Prompt Injection Protection includes safeguards against attempts to override instructions via malicious text — a direct match for defenses against prompt injection in tool/web content.
Security Guidance Plugin makes Claude review its own code changes for vulnerabilities — directly realizing security scanning of agent/code actions against a threat model.

Dev Container Integration runs Claude Code inside a Docker container managed by VS Code with the project mounted — a direct match for running the agent inside a defined dev container/isolated runtime.
OpenTelemetry Monitoring exports metrics, events, and traces via OpenTelemetry — directly realizing audit logging/telemetry for review and compliance.
Automated PR Code Review uses multi-agent analysis to flag logic errors and security vulnerabilities in PRs — a direct match for automated code review of diffs/PRs before they land.
Cost Tracking tracks spend and per-session token accounting — directly realizing cost and usage tracking.

Mid-Session Workflow Resume resumes a paused workflow within the same session reusing cached agent results. This is context caching/continuation within a session, not resuming or forking a previous (ended) session — the capability specifically targets cross-session resume or branching.
Remote Control lets you continue a local Claude Code session from a phone, tablet, or browser — directly realizing remote control from mobile/web clients.
Mobile Push Notifications sends push alerts to mobile devices for remote session events and updates — a direct match for push notifications on run progress/completion.
Claude Code in Slack lets users delegate coding tasks from a Slack workspace by mentioning @Claude — directly realizing driving the agent from a chat surface.

Multi-Surface Connector Support describes where connectors (integrations) are available, not where the agent itself can be used. The capability is about agent access surfaces (terminal, IDE, web, mobile); connectors being available across surfaces is adjacent/infrastructure, not the same as the agent's own access surface definition.
